summary: Russian equipment positioned on the Belarusian border is being used to strike Ukrainian energy and railway targets in Zhytomyr, Rivne and Volyn regions. Russian forces have intensified missile and UAV attacks across multiple fronts, including KAB strikes on Zaporizhzhia and artillery bombardments on Sumy. Ukrainian authorities report civilian casualties and infrastructure damage in several oblasts.
key_points: Russian equipment on the Belarusian border is striking Zhytomyr, Rivne and Volyn regions, targeting energy and railway infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
Russians shelled Sloviansk with RSZV rockets, damaging multi‑storey buildings and injuring two women and one man (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
An artillery strike on Sumy damaged private homes, apartments and vehicles, resulting in six injured civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82087).
Nine KAB attacks on Zaporizhzhia caused four deaths, six injuries and damage to at least 20 houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
KABs were also reported in the Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/65858).
Groups of reactive UAVs were observed moving from Chernihiv toward the Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65864).
Multiple Shahed drones were detected over Odesa and Izmail areas, with at least 20 passing near Vilkove (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67961).
Ukrainian officials warned of a possible mass air strike by Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).

sections.executive_summary: Russian equipment deployed on the Belarusian border is being used to strike Ukrainian energy and railway targets in Zhytomyr, Rivne and Volyn. Russian missile, artillery and UAV attacks have caused civilian casualties and extensive damage in Sloviansk, Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and other oblasts. Ukrainian authorities warn of an imminent mass air strike and increased tactical aviation activity.

points: In Sloviansk, Russian RSZV rockets damaged multi‑storey residential buildings, injuring two women (aged 30 and 89) and a 31‑year‑old man (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
An artillery barrage on Sumy destroyed private houses, apartment blocks and vehicles, leaving six civilians injured and prompting a full emergency response (Source: @UkraineNow/82087).
Nine KAB strikes on Zaporizhzhia resulted in four fatalities, six injuries and damage to at least 20 private dwellings (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Earlier KAB attacks near Sumy caused one civilian death, adding to the cumulative casualty count in the region (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Ukrainian officials reported that four Russian‑controlled relay stations in Belarus (Gomel and Brest regions) are being used to target energy and railway infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
Russian forces are operating four relay stations in Belarus, specifically in the Gomel and Brest regions, to conduct strikes on Ukrainian energy and rail assets (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
KAB missiles have been launched against Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk oblasts, indicating a broadened missile campaign (Source: @kpszsu/65858).
Russian tactical aviation activity has increased on the north‑east and south‑east axes, posing heightened threat to front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
A mass air strike is being prepared by Russian forces, according to statements from the Ukrainian president (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
Fuel shipments from Belarus to Russia have risen dramatically, suggesting logistical support for Russian operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
Ukrainian Sumy oblast suffered an artillery strike that damaged residential structures and injured six civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82087).
Kharkiv region observed a reactive UAV heading toward Bohodukhiv, indicating continued aerial pressure from the north (Source: @kpszsu/65912).
Donetsk oblast reported a UAV moving northward toward Dnipro, suggesting cross‑border reconnaissance or strike intent (Source: @kpszsu/65915).
Shahed drones were sighted over the eastern approaches of Sumy, with one drone reported approaching from the east (Source: @kpszsu/65915).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over the eastern sector of Kharkiv heading toward Shevchenko and Chuhuyiv (Source: @kpszsu/65915).
A group of reactive UAVs launched from Chernihiv oblast is heading toward the Kyiv region, targeting the capital (Source: @kpszsu/65864).
Kinjal missiles were observed traveling from Chernihiv to the Vyshhorod district of Kyiv, indicating missile use on the northern approach (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67971).
UAV activity was reported near Kyiv, with a UAV passing over the city at 11:11 and a separate alert at 12:18, reflecting ongoing aerial threats (Source: @eRadarrua/97623; @eRadarrua/97643).
Shahed drones were detected circling north of Rivne, suggesting reconnaissance over the north‑western front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67962).
Ukrainian air defenses reported increased activity of enemy tactical aviation on the north‑east axis, heightening risk to front‑line positions (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
Multiple UAVs were tracked moving from Mykolaiv oblast toward the north‑west, including one near Novo Bugh and another passing Velyka Oleksandrivka in Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/65878).
UAVs from the north of Kherson oblast are heading toward Mykolaiv and Bereznegovatoye, indicating pressure on southern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/65881).
A reactive UAV was observed near Kazanka in Mykolaiv moving northward, adding to the aerial activity in the south (Source: @kpszsu/65908).
Shahed drones were reported over the Odesa region, with at least 20 passing near Vilkove in the Izmail/Kiliya area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67961).
Additional Shahed drones were sighted over Izmail, reinforcing the intensity of UAV attacks in the southern theater (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67962).
Ukrainian authorities warned of an imminent mass air strike by Russian forces, citing preparation for large‑scale aerial attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
Russian tactical aviation is active on north‑east and south‑east fronts, increasing the threat to front‑line oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
UAV sightings across multiple regions—including Kyiv, Mykolaiv, Kherson, Kharkiv and Donetsk—demonstrate a widespread aerial campaign (Source: multiple).
Shahed drone activity has intensified in the south, with clusters observed over Odesa and Izmail, indicating a focused UAV offensive (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67961; @mon1tor_ua/67962).
KAB missile launches against Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk highlight the use of precision strike assets alongside UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/65858).

key_developments: Four Russian‑controlled relay stations in Belarus (Gomel and Brest) are being used to strike Ukrainian energy and railway targets in Zhytomyr, Rivne and Volyn (Source: @UkraineNow/82081).
Russian RSZV rockets hit Sloviansk, damaging multi‑storey buildings and injuring three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
Artillery fire on Sumy destroyed private homes and vehicles, leaving six civilians injured (Source: @UkraineNow/82087).
Nine KAB attacks on Zaporizhzhia caused four deaths, six injuries and destroyed at least 20 houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Groups of reactive UAVs launched from Chernihiv are heading toward Kyiv, while Kinjal missiles were tracked moving from Chernihiv to Vyshhorod (Source: @kpszsu/65864; @mon1tor_ua/67971).
Shahed drones concentrated over Odesa and Izmail, with at least 20 observed near Vilkove, indicating a focused southern UAV offensive (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67961).
Ukrainian officials warned of a mass Russian air strike, and tactical aviation activity increased on north‑east and south‑east axes (Source: @UkraineNow/82081; @kpszsu/65882).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are leveraging Belarusian relay stations to extend their strike range against Ukrainian critical infrastructure, while intensifying missile, artillery and UAV attacks across multiple fronts. The surge in KAB and Shahed drone usage, combined with preparations for a mass air strike, elevates the risk of high‑casualty events and infrastructure disruption. Ukrainian air defenses face a multi‑vector threat, requiring allocation of resources to both high‑altitude aircraft and low‑altitude UAV interceptions.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine has heightened alert levels in affected oblasts, mobilizing emergency services and reinforcing air defense units around Kyiv, Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and southern coastal areas. Coordination with NATO partners is expected to focus on intelligence sharing regarding Belarusian relay stations and increased UAV activity. Civilian authorities are advising populations in high‑risk zones to seek shelter during air raid alerts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of the four Belarusian relay sites should be prioritized to assess their operational status and potential for neutralization. UAV flight paths indicate a pattern of targeting logistics corridors in the south and command centers in the north, suggesting a strategic aim to disrupt supply lines and command‑control. The reported increase in fuel shipments from Belarus to Russia may reflect logistical support for sustained offensive operations.
